proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_035=1 have a higher objective_response rate than patients with feature_035=0.

analyses.code:
from scipy import stats
import pandas as pd
tab = pd.crosstab(df['feature_035'], df['objective_response'])
stats.chi2_contingency(tab)
analyses.result_summary: Patients with feature_035=1 (n=3624) had an objective_response rate of 0.2213 vs 0.1650 in feature_035=0 (n=46376). Absolute difference +0.0563 (chi-square p=3.5e-18). Strongly significant positive association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher levels of the 3-category ordinal variable feature_057 are associated with lower objective_response rates (monotonic decrease across feature_057 = 0, 1, 2).

analyses.code:
import statsmodels.formula.api as smf
smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_057', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Response rates monotonically decrease across feature_057 levels: 0.2129 (level 0, n=17592), 0.1552 (level 1, n=24971), 0.1119 (level 2, n=7437). Logistic-regression slope per unit increase in feature_057 = -0.384 (chi-square overall p=5.5e-98; slope p=1.8e-97). Strongest single prognostic variable identified.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of the continuous laboratory variable feature_011 are associated with lower probability of objective_response.

analyses.code:
from scipy import stats
stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df.objective_response==1,'feature_011'], df.loc[df.objective_response==0,'feature_011'])
import statsmodels.formula.api as smf
smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_011', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_011 = 3.38 in responders vs 3.95 in non-responders (Welch t-test p=7.2e-39). Univariable logistic regression beta = -0.040 per unit increase (p=7.4e-35). Strong inverse association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of feature_006 are associated with lower probability of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_006 = 58.23 in responders vs 60.31 in non-responders (Welch t p=9.5e-20). Logistic beta = -0.0057 per unit (p=1.2e-19). Significant inverse association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Older patient age (continuous variable feature_078, range 18-92, mean ~68) is associated with a lower probability of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_078 = 68.03 in responders vs 67.98 in non-responders (essentially identical). Univariable logistic beta = +0.0004/year (p=0.72). NOT significant; age is not associated with response in this cohort, contrary to the common AML expectation.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_063 is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher feature_092 is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher feature_099 (range 1.7-5.5) is associated with higher objective_response.
Higher feature_084 is associated with higher objective_response.
feature_055 is associated with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_063 = 5.65 in responders vs 6.17 in non-responders. Univariable logistic beta = -0.0070 (p=2.8e-6). Significant inverse association.
Mean feature_092 = 19.07 in responders vs 20.40 in non-responders. Univariable logistic beta = -0.0022 (p=1.9e-5). Significant inverse association.
Mean feature_099 = 3.825 in responders vs 3.796 in non-responders. Univariable logistic beta = +0.115 per unit (p=1.4e-6). Significant positive association as predicted.
Mean feature_084 = 105.29 in responders vs 104.13 in non-responders. Univariable logistic beta = +0.00050 (p=0.048). Marginally significant; very small effect.
Mean feature_055 = 223.07 in responders vs 223.97 in non-responders. Univariable logistic beta = -0.0001 (p=0.35). NOT significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Objective_response rates differ by self-reported race (feature_005); specifically, white patients have a higher response rate than non-white patients.

analyses.code:
import pandas as pd
from scipy import stats
tab = pd.crosstab(df['feature_005'], df['objective_response'])
stats.chi2_contingency(tab)
df['race_white'] = (df['feature_005']=='white').astype(int)
smf.logit('objective_response ~ race_white', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Response rates by race: white 0.1728 (n=32498), hispanic 0.1670, asian 0.1633, black 0.1574, other 0.1472. Overall chi-square p=0.0096. White vs non-white: logistic beta = +0.078 (OR 1.081, p=0.002). Significant positive association of white race with response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Objective_response rate differs by insurance category (feature_087: medicaid, medicare, private, uninsured).

analyses.result_summary: Response rates: medicare 0.1718, medicaid 0.1687, private 0.1666, uninsured 0.1662. Chi-square p=0.54. NOT significant; insurance category is not associated with response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After mutual adjustment, feature_035, feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_063, feature_092, feature_099, and race_white remain independently associated with objective_response, while age (feature_078) does not.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_035 + feature_057 + feature_011 + feature_006 + feature_063 + feature_092 + feature_099 + feature_078 + race_white', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: All eight clinical predictors retain significance after mutual adjustment with similar magnitudes to univariable estimates (feature_035 beta=+0.36, feature_057 beta=-0.38, feature_011 beta=-0.040, feature_006 beta=-0.006, feature_063 beta=-0.007, feature_092 beta=-0.002, feature_099 beta=+0.118, race_white beta=+0.076; all p<0.005). Age (feature_078) remains non-significant (beta=+0.0004, p=0.74). McFadden pseudo-R^2 modest (~0.024).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of feature_035=1 on objective_response is larger in patients with lower feature_057 (i.e., feature_035 x feature_057 interaction; treatment works better in the lower-risk stratum).

analyses.result_summary: Stratified absolute differences: feature_057=0 +0.062, =1 +0.053, =2 +0.044. Logistic interaction term feature_035:feature_057 beta=+0.027 (p=0.674). NOT significant; the treatment-like effect of feature_035 is roughly constant on the log-odds scale across feature_057 strata.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_035 on objective_response is larger in white patients than in non-white patients (treatment x race interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Stratified absolute differences: white +0.065 (n=32498), non-white +0.042 (n=17502). Interaction term feature_035:race_white beta=+0.123 (p=0.167). Direction matches hypothesis but NOT statistically significant; insufficient evidence for a race-modified treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of feature_035 is larger in patients with lower feature_011 (i.e., interaction such that the negative biomarker feature_011 attenuates treatment benefit).

analyses.result_summary: feature_035:feature_011 interaction beta=-0.014 (p=0.225). NOT significant. The benefit of feature_035 does not vary measurably with feature_011.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_035 on objective_response varies with patient age (feature_078).

analyses.result_summary: feature_035:feature_078 interaction beta=-0.0004/year (p=0.92). NOT significant. The effect of feature_035 is independent of age.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The strong negative association of feature_057 with objective_response is robust to adjustment for the candidate treatment feature_035 (i.e., feature_057 is not merely a proxy for differential treatment receipt).

analyses.result_summary: feature_057 beta=-0.383 (essentially unchanged from univariable -0.384, p=2e-97); feature_035 beta=+0.358 (p=2e-17). Both effects act independently; feature_057 is a true prognostic marker, not confounded by feature_035.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher levels of the 5-level ordinal variable feature_096 are associated with higher (or lower) objective_response.
Higher levels of the 5-level ordinal variable feature_064 are associated with objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Response rates by feature_096 level: 0.169, 0.166, 0.175, 0.164, 0.177 (no clear monotonic pattern). Logistic slope beta=+0.007 per unit (p=0.51). NOT significant.
Response rates by feature_064 level: 0.169, 0.167, 0.168, 0.173, 0.180 (slight upward trend at higher levels but small). Logistic slope beta=+0.013 (p=0.21). NOT significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher values of the 11-level count-like variable feature_018 (range 0-10) are associated with lower objective_response (consistent with a comorbidity burden interpretation).

analyses.result_summary: Response rates by feature_018 level range from 0.163 to 0.190 with no clear monotonic pattern. Slope beta=+0.0042 (p=0.34). NOT significant; feature_018 does not predict response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the highest-risk stratum (feature_057=2), patients receiving feature_035=1 still have higher objective_response than feature_035=0.

analyses.result_summary: In feature_057=2 subgroup (n=7437), feature_035=1 rr=0.1531 (n=516) vs feature_035=0 rr=0.1088 (n=6921), absolute diff +0.044, beta=+0.39 (p=0.0022). Significant; feature_035 retains a meaningful benefit even in the highest-risk subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_093=1 have a higher objective_response rate than feature_093=0.

analyses.result_summary: feature_093=1 (n=4025) rr=0.1848 vs =0 (n=45975) rr=0.1677. Absolute diff +0.017, chi-square p=0.0056. Significant but small positive association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher levels of ordinal variable feature_042 are associated with lower objective_response.
Higher levels of ordinal variable feature_045 are associated with lower objective_response.
Higher levels of ordinal variable feature_125 are associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: Logistic slope beta=-0.010 per unit (p=0.35). NOT significant.
Logistic slope beta=-0.020 (p=0.070). Marginal/borderline; direction matches hypothesis but does not reach 0.05.
Logistic slope beta=-0.023 (p=0.038). Significant inverse association in the predicted direction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There exists at least one binary feature B such that the effect of feature_035 on objective_response is substantially modified by B (treatment-by-biomarker interaction).

analyses.code:
for c in binary_features:
    smf.logit(f'objective_response ~ feature_035 * {c}', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Systematic scan of 80 binary features for feature_035 x feature interactions. Two interactions are highly significant: feature_035 x feature_027 (interaction beta=+1.05, p=1.1e-32) and feature_035 x feature_014 (interaction beta=+0.73, p=7.7e-18). Several smaller interactions (feature_058, feature_124, feature_013, feature_073) also reached p<0.05 but are likely chance findings given multiplicity. The two top hits are far above the multiplicity threshold (Bonferroni p<0.05/80 = 6.3e-4) and warrant focused follow-up.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_035 increases objective_response only in patients with feature_027=1; in patients with feature_027=0, feature_035 has no effect (predictive biomarker hypothesis).

analyses.result_summary: Stratified analysis: feature_027=0 subgroup (n=34986): feature_035=1 rr=0.1680 (n=2577) vs =0 rr=0.1672 (n=32409), diff +0.0008, chi-square p=0.94 — NO benefit. feature_027=1 subgroup (n=15014): feature_035=1 rr=0.3524 (n=1047) vs =0 rr=0.1597 (n=13967), diff +0.193, chi-square p=1.4e-56 — large benefit. Hypothesis strongly supported: feature_027 is a positive predictive biomarker for feature_035.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_035 increases objective_response only in patients with feature_014=1; in patients with feature_014=0, feature_035 has no effect.

analyses.result_summary: Stratified analysis: feature_014=0 (n=29980): feature_035=1 rr=0.1713 (n=2189) vs =0 rr=0.1657 (n=27791), diff +0.0056, chi-square p=0.51 — NO benefit. feature_014=1 (n=20020): feature_035=1 rr=0.2976 (n=1435) vs =0 rr=0.1639 (n=18585), diff +0.134, chi-square p=8.9e-38 — large benefit. Hypothesis supported: feature_014 is also a positive predictive biomarker for feature_035.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_035 produces a clinically meaningful increase in objective_response only in the joint-positive subgroup (feature_027=1 AND feature_014=1); in any other combination, feature_035 has essentially no effect (3-way interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Joint stratification reveals a striking pattern. Response rate by (feature_027, feature_014, feature_035): (0,0,0)=0.169 vs (0,0,1)=0.173, diff +0.004; (0,1,0)=0.165 vs (0,1,1)=0.161, diff -0.004; (1,0,0)=0.159 vs (1,0,1)=0.168, diff +0.009; (1,1,0)=0.161 vs (1,1,1)=0.621, diff +0.461 (n=427 treated). The 3-way interaction term feature_035:feature_027:feature_014 has beta=+2.144 (z=11.23, p~6e-29). Of all six lower-order terms in the same model, only this 3-way term is significant. Interpretation: feature_035 is a precision therapy whose benefit is essentially confined to the doubly biomarker-positive subset (~12% of the cohort).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_011 above the cohort median (~3.0) have lower objective_response than those at or below median.

A multivariable logistic model built from feature_035, feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_063, feature_092, feature_099, and race_white can usefully discriminate responders from non-responders (AUC > 0.55) and produces a monotonic gradient in observed response across deciles of predicted probability.

analyses.result_summary: Median feature_011 = 3.00. High-feature_011 (n=24862) rr=0.1522 vs low (n=25138) rr=0.1857; absolute diff -0.033, chi-square p=2.4e-23. Strong inverse association confirmed at the dichotomized level.

analyses.code:
m = smf.logit('objective_response ~ feature_035 + feature_057 + feature_011 + feature_006 + feature_063 + feature_092 + feature_099 + race_white', data=df).fit()
pred = m.predict(df)
from scipy.stats import mannwhitneyu
mannwhitneyu(pred[y==1], pred[y==0], alternative='greater')
analyses.result_summary: AUC = 0.597 (Mann-Whitney p=5.6e-175). Observed response rate by decile of predicted probability rises monotonically: 0.087, 0.123, 0.136, 0.149, 0.155, 0.173, 0.184, 0.201, 0.228, 0.254 (lowest to highest decile). Discrimination is modest but the calibration gradient is clean and significant; an even stronger model would incorporate the feature_027 x feature_014 x feature_035 interaction identified earlier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The white-vs-non-white association with objective_response (h7) is at least partly confounded by clinical predictors and will be attenuated to non-significance after adjusting for feature_035, feature_057, feature_011, feature_006, feature_063, feature_092, feature_099, and feature_078.
After adjusting for clinical covariates, the benefit of feature_035 differs by race (race_white x feature_035 interaction).

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ race_white + feature_035 + feature_057 + feature_011 + feature_006 + feature_063 + feature_092 + feature_099 + feature_078', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: race_white beta=+0.0764 (OR 1.079, p=0.003) — virtually unchanged from the unadjusted estimate (+0.078, p=0.002). The white-vs-non-white association is NOT explained by the measured clinical covariates; hypothesis 25a is REFUTED. A small but persistent ~1% absolute difference in response associated with white race remains and warrants further scrutiny for unmeasured confounding or potential equity issues.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ race_white * feature_035 + feature_057 + feature_011 + feature_006 + feature_063 + feature_092 + feature_099', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: race_white:feature_035 interaction beta=+0.133 (p=0.138) after full adjustment. NOT statistically significant; no convincing evidence that feature_035 benefit varies by race once clinical covariates are accounted for.
